title: NZ School Holiday Dates 2025–2027 — Planning Guide
meta_desc: Complete list of NZ school holidays 2025–2027 by region — Auckland, Wellington, Christchurch, Hamilton, Tauranga. Includes teacher-only days, Easter dates, and school websites.
intro: New Zealand has four mandatory school holiday blocks per year, plus regional variations and teacher-only days that differ by region. This guide covers exact dates for 2025–2027 broken down by region, Easter variations, and which days teachers are working but students aren't. Use this to plan your family trips, book accommodation, and understand why Zealandia night tours (Wellington) and ski lifts (Queenstown) book out months ahead.
key_takeaway: All NZ regions follow the same four school holidays (April, July, October, December–January), but start/end dates vary by region by 1–2 weeks. Teacher-only days (5–8 per year) differ by region too. July = ski season peak. Summer = beach season, premium pricing, bookings needed 2–3 months ahead. Easter moves annually — 2025: April 20, 2026: April 5, 2027: March 28.

sections.heading: National School Holiday Blocks 2025–2027
sections.body: New Zealand schools have four mandatory holiday periods per year. All regions share the same approximate dates, but Auckland, Wellington, and Christchurch stagger their start/end dates by 1–2 weeks. This guide provides exact dates for the major regions.

rows: Golden weather, low crowds, mild temps 15–20°C
Good for wine regions, Hawke's Bay, Rotorua (fewer queues)
Queenstown, Wanaka, Ruapehu busiest; book 3+ months ahead
Warming up 12–18°C, spring flowers, shoulder season
Abel Tasman, Nelson, Bay of Islands; moderate crowds, good deals
Peak beach season, hot 20–28°C, school holiday premium pricing
Coromandel, Northland, Bay of Islands; book 2–3 months ahead, expect crowds

sections.heading: Quick Reference — All 2026 NZ School Holiday Dates
sections.body: Here's every 2026 school holiday in one table. Dates are nationwide approximations — Auckland starts Term 1 one day earlier, and Canterbury (Christchurch) often starts the summer break a week earlier. Always confirm with your child's school.

rows: Campervans by Sep, parks by Aug

sections.heading: 2026 NZ School Term Dates
sections.body: NZ schools run four terms a year. These are the 2026 term dates (Auckland begins Term 1 a day earlier due to Auckland Anniversary). Term 4 end dates are approximate and confirmed by each school late in the year.

sections.heading: Public Holidays That Affect Travel in 2026
sections.body: Public holidays change what's open, push up accommodation demand, and create long weekends families travel on. The big ones to plan around in 2026:
list: Waitangi Day — Fri 6 Feb 2026 (long weekend, beaches busy)
Good Friday — Fri 3 Apr 2026 (restricted trading; most shops shut, supermarkets closed)
Easter Sunday — Sun 5 Apr 2026 (restricted trading; falls inside autumn school holidays)
Easter Monday — Mon 6 Apr 2026 (shops trade normally)
Anzac Day — Sat 25 Apr 2026 (restricted trading until 1pm; near end of autumn break)
King's Birthday — Mon 1 Jun 2026 (long weekend, pre-ski-season getaways)
Matariki — Fri 10 Jul 2026 (long weekend, falls inside winter school holidays)
Labour Day — Mon 26 Oct 2026 (long weekend, end of spring season)
Christmas Day & Boxing Day — Fri 25 / Sat 26 Dec 2026 (restricted trading; peak summer crowds begin)

sections.heading: Easter Dates (Moving Holiday) 2025–2027
sections.body: Easter dates move every year based on the lunar calendar. Schools don't close specifically for Easter (it falls during school holiday periods), but Easter holidays often straddle school term dates. Check school websites for Easter Monday variations by region.

rows: Falls within April school holidays (most regions already off)
Falls within April school holidays (most regions already off)
Falls during term time for most regions — some may close specifically for Easter

sections.heading: Teacher-Only Days by Region (Non-Student Days)
sections.body: Teachers work on these days, but students are not in school. These vary by region and by school. Most regions have 5–8 teacher-only days per year, typically at the start or end of term.
sections.body_extra:
Auckland & Waikato (Hamilton, Tauranga)

Teacher-only days typically scheduled: early July (before ski holiday), early October (before spring holiday), late January (after summer), plus 1–2 term-start days in February. Check individual school websites — these vary.

Wellington

Same pattern as Auckland. Wellington schools may add an extra teacher day in September. Check Victoria University school partnership websites for specific dates.

Christchurch & Canterbury

Christchurch schools often have teacher-only days: late January, mid-February (term start), early October. Christchurch may shift slightly to accommodate South Island travel logistics.

rows: Start of terms, plus extra in October

sections.heading: Plan Each 2026 Holiday — Activity Guides & Booking Windows
sections.body:
Each NZ school holiday has its own character, weather, and booking rhythm. Here's how to plan each one, with links to our detailed activity guides for every period.

Winter (July) — peak ski season. July is the busiest, most expensive holiday for snow travel. Queenstown, Wanaka, and Ruapehu fill first; book accommodation 3–4 months ahead (April for July travel) and buy ski lift passes before you arrive. The Skyline luge in Queenstown can hit 45-minute waits mid-morning — go at 9am opening or after 3pm. Wellington's Zealandia night tours (only three per night) sell out 6+ weeks ahead. See our full NZ winter school holidays activities guide, plus city-specific picks for Auckland, Wellington, and Christchurch winter 2026.

Summer (December–January) — the big one. Six weeks long and overlapping Christmas and New Year. Coastal destinations book out 8–12 weeks ahead; the Coromandel and Bay of Islands fill 3+ months in advance. Campervans sell out by September and Top 10 holiday parks by August. Expect premium pricing — accommodation can be 2–3× normal cost over Boxing Day to early January. Start planning now with our summer school holidays 2026/27 guide.

Autumn (April) — best value, settled weather. Mild 15–20°C days, golden light, and far fewer crowds. Hawke's Bay, Rotorua, and the wine regions shine. Accommodation books 4–6 weeks ahead rather than months. Easter falls inside this break in 2026 — see our NZ Easter holiday ideas and Easter 2027 guide.

Spring (September–October) — the underrated one. Warming up (12–18°C), thinning crowds, and lower prices. Abel Tasman, Nelson, and the top of the South Island are ideal. Book 4–6 weeks ahead for the best value-to-experience ratio of the year.

For a calendar view of every holiday with destination suggestions, see the NZ school holidays hub. Or use our interactive school holiday activity finder to filter 70+ ideas by city, age group and season.

sections.heading: Regional School Website Links for Confirmation
sections.body: Official school and education authority websites for each region. Check these for teacher-only days, specific term dates, and any regional variations.
list: Auckland Council Schools — https://www.auckland.govt.nz/en/about-auckland/how-auckland-council-works/projects-and-initiatives/schools.html
Wellington Region Schools — https://www.nzei.org.nz (Education union site, has term dates)
Christchurch & Canterbury Schools — https://www.ccc.govt.nz/learning-and-culture/schools/ (Christchurch City Council education listings)
Waikato/Hamilton Schools — https://www.waikatoregion.govt.nz (Waikato Regional Council)
Tauranga & Bay of Plenty Schools — https://www.bopdhb.govt.nz (Bay of Plenty District Health Board, also education info)
NZ Education Ministry — https://www.education.govt.nz (national school calendar and term dates)
Te Kura (online NZ school) — https://tekura.school.nz (has national holiday calendar)

sections.heading: Key Dates to Remember When Booking
sections.body: Use these rules to book family travel at the right time:
list: Book accommodation 2–3 months ahead for summer (Dec–Jan) and July (ski season)
Book accommodation 4–6 weeks ahead for autumn (April) and spring (October)
Book experiences (Zealandia night tours, Te Papa programmes, ski lift passes) even earlier — some require 6–8 weeks' notice
Popular attractions offer school holiday programme bookings online 6+ weeks ahead — check websites when the term dates are announced
Zealandia (Wellington), Te Papa (Wellington), and ski lift passes should be booked immediately when dates are confirmed
Summer holiday parks book out Christmas–New Year; book by September for December travel
July = ski season; every activity involving snow or altitude is busiest during school holidays
Easter dates move annually — check school calendars, as some regions may close for Easter even outside formal holidays

sections.heading: Free Things to Do With Kids — By City
sections.body:
School holidays don't have to be expensive. Every major NZ city has excellent free activities for kids — playgrounds, museums, beaches, parks, and local events. We've put together free-activities guides for all 15 main NZ cities:

Free things to do in Auckland · Wellington · Christchurch · Hamilton · Tauranga · Dunedin · Rotorua · Napier · New Plymouth · Palmerston North · Whangārei · Gisborne · Taupō · Queenstown · Nelson

Or use our interactive school holiday activity finder to filter 70+ ideas by city, age group, and season.

sections.heading: Regional Variations (Why Dates Differ)
sections.body:
New Zealand schools are divided into five regional areas, each with slightly different holiday dates. This is managed by the Ministry of Education to spread holidays evenly and reduce strain on attractions during peak periods. The regions are:

- **Region 1:** Auckland, Waikato (including Hamilton), Bay of Plenty (Tauranga), Rotorua, Taupo
- **Region 2:** Wellington, Manawatu, Wairarapa, Hawke's Bay
- **Region 3:** Taranaki, Nelson, Marlborough
- **Region 4:** West Coast, Canterbury (including Christchurch)
- **Region 5:** Otago (Dunedin, Queenstown), Southland (Invercargill)

Region 1 and Region 2 typically differ by 1–2 weeks. Region 4 (Christchurch, Canterbury) often starts summer holidays earlier (late November vs early December) to extend their break. Always check your child's specific school website for exact dates.

faq.question: Do all NZ schools have the same holiday dates?
faq.answer: Almost — NZ divides schools into 5 regional areas with slightly staggered dates. Auckland, Wellington, and Christchurch differ by 1–2 weeks. All regions share the same four holiday blocks (April, July, October, Dec–Jan), but start/end dates vary. Always check your child's school website for exact dates.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit NZ with school-age kids?
faq.answer: Autumn (April school holidays) or spring (October school holidays) offer the best balance — fewer crowds, good weather, and moderate pricing. July has great skiing but is busiest and most expensive. Summer (Dec–Jan) is beach season but extremely crowded and expensive. If visiting from overseas, autumn and spring are most family-friendly.

faq.question: When is Easter in 2026 and 2027?
faq.answer: Easter 2026: April 5 (falls within school holidays in most regions). Easter 2027: March 29 (falls during term time for most regions — some schools may close). Easter is determined by the lunar calendar and moves every year.

faq.question: How far ahead should I book activities during school holidays?
faq.answer: For July (ski season) and Dec–Jan (summer): book 2–3 months ahead and popular experiences 6–8 weeks ahead. For April and October: book 4–6 weeks ahead. Zealandia night tours (Wellington) need 6+ weeks' notice. Ski lift passes should be booked before you arrive in July.

faq.question: Are teacher-only days the same as school holidays?
faq.answer: No — teacher-only days are when teachers work but students don't attend (5–8 per year). School holidays are when the whole school closes. Teachers use these days for professional development and planning. Teacher-only days vary by region and sometimes by individual school.

faq.question: Can I find the exact dates for my child's school?
faq.answer: Yes — check your school's website directly, or contact them by email. You can also check the Ministry of Education website (https://www.education.govt.nz) for regional calendar information. Individual schools sometimes add extra teacher-only days or term breaks.
